scenes.title: Portrait — golden hour
scenes.alt: Portrait subject standing outdoors at golden hour with a receding background and street movement.

presets.label: Default exposure
presets.description: Balanced portrait settings with clean detail.

presets.label: Soft background
presets.description: Wide aperture for stronger subject separation.

presets.label: Deep focus
presets.description: Narrow aperture keeps more of the scene sharp.

presets.label: Noisy high ISO
presets.description: High ISO brightens the frame and adds grain.

lessons.title: Portraiture
lessons.summary: Learn how aperture changes background blur, how shutter speed handles movement, and how ISO affects exposure and grain.

steps.title: Separate the subject
steps.body: A wide aperture keeps the subject prominent while softening the background. It is a common portrait choice when the environment should support the subject without competing for attention.

steps.title: Keep the location readable
steps.body: A narrower aperture brings more background detail back into the frame. Use it when the setting tells part of the story.

steps.title: Freeze movement
steps.body: A fast shutter speed reduces motion blur. It is useful when the subject, camera, or background activity is moving.

steps.title: Trade cleanliness for brightness
steps.body: Higher ISO helps brighten an image when the aperture or shutter speed cannot change, but the image becomes noisier.
